steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question asking what is true about Dorian based on the passage. The passage describes Dorian's physical and emotional reaction upon first seeing Morgan's portrait of him. The correct answer must be directly supported by the text — no outside inference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — He wants to know Morgan's opinion of the portrait.: The passage says Dorian was 'dimly conscious that Morgan was speaking to him, but not catching the meaning of his words.' Dorian is absorbed in his own reaction, not concerned with Morgan's view. ✗
  • Choice B — He is delighted by what he sees in the portrait.: His cheeks 'flushed for a moment with pleasure,' a 'look of joy came into his eyes,' and the sense of his own beauty struck him 'like a revelation.' The text directly and repeatedly signals delight. ✓
  • Choice C — He prefers portraits to other types of paintings.: The text makes no comparison between portrait and other art forms. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — He is uncertain of Morgan's talent as an artist.: Nothing in the passage suggests doubt about Morgan's skill; Dorian's reaction is entirely focused on seeing his own beauty, not evaluating the painter. ✗
That is the correct answer.
